description_long: A loosely clustered pile of blue-tinted plastic garbage bags is shown, each bag appearing semi-translucent and containing indistinct, multicolored refuse. The bags are irregularly shaped, with some bulging more than others, and a few smaller fragments or pieces are scattered nearby. The plastic material is thin and slightly glossy, with visible creases and folds that catch the light. Hints of red, white, and dark blue are visible through the plastic, suggesting mixed waste inside. No visible closures or ties are apparent, and the bags rest directly on the ground, overlapping each other. The overall appearance is that of discarded street trash, commonly found in urban environments.
description: A pile of semi-translucent blue plastic garbage bags, loosely clustered with visible multicolored waste inside and scattered fragments nearby.
description_view_0: From this angle, the largest bag is prominent in the foreground, with a small detached piece to the left and subtle hints of red and dark blue visible through the plastic.
description_view_1: This view highlights the irregular shapes and overlapping arrangement of the bags, with more blue tones and a clearer view of the mixed contents inside.
description_view_2: The bags appear more spread out, with several smaller fragments visible on the right and the semi-translucent plastic revealing more of the internal refuse.
description_view_3: A rear angle shows the pile's depth, with a few isolated pieces in the foreground and the bags' creased surfaces reflecting light.
attribute_fullness_or_bulging: moderately full and irregularly bulging, with some bags more compressed than others
